{"key_event":"Brexit sovereignty and COVID-era executive powers","reasoning":"Post-Brexit autonomy, emergency COVID powers, and new public-order/security/online laws modestly increased state authority, though fiscal stress, public-service backlogs, devolution tensions, and legitimacy shocks limited the gain. Civil society remained exceptionally strong through courts, elections, media scrutiny, protests, unions, and devolved institutions, but tighter protest regulation, executive centralization, polarization, and misinformation slightly reduced its effective checking power.","society_change":-0.1,"society_power":9.3,"state_change":0.1,"state_power":9.2}
